div.p: ConflictTerm/ConflictIndex reduces round trips from O(log length) to O(1)
Problem: If follower is 1000 entries behind, this requires 1000 round trips!
Benefit: Even if follower is 10,000 entries behind, catch-up happens in ~2-3 round trips.

// Find first index with this term

div.p: Follower's log[5].term = 2 Leader sent PrevLogTerm = 2 ✓ Match! But follower has no entry 6 to compare...
Follower's log[5].term = 2 Leader sends entry {2:5} Terms match! No conflict... But we still retry blindly.
Finally succeeds! But required 3 round trips for just 3 entries difference.
Follower's log[5].term = 3 Leader sent PrevLogTerm = 2 ✗ Mismatch! Follower sends:

ol.li: If not found → nextIndex = ConflictIndex In this case: log[6].term = 3, but follower only has entries 1-5. The divergence is at index 4 where follower has term 3 entry.

div.p: Leader jumps directly to the right index using conflict information. 1 round trip instead of 3!

text.tspan: // Or if no match: nextIndex = ConflictIndex
